name: M9 minimal medium + 1% (w/v) glucose (PMC4893254)
description: M9 minimal medium + 1% (w/v) glucose — medium reported in PMC4893254 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC4893254 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'M9' expanded to standard composition: M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell 2001): Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2 + carbon source.
provenance.verification_evidence: M9 medium, supplemented with 1 % (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS and 50 mg/L tryptophan, was used for assessing growth of genome-reduced strains [46].
provenance.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (Bacillus subtilis formulation per Harwood & Cutting 1990) + 1% (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS, 50 mg/L L-tryptophan

sources_chased.what: paper main text (PMC4893254 Methods) for the exact supplementation over M9
base M9 recipe cited as ref [46] = Harwood CR, Cutting SM. Molecular biological methods for Bacillus. Wiley, 1990
sources_chased.how: reference identified in fullTextXML (CR46); standard B. subtilis M9 recipe recovered from SubtiWiki M9 page, which cites the same Harwood & Cutting 1990 source

recuration.notes: Organism is Bacillus subtilis (BSF1 and genome-reduced derivatives); cultivation was strictly aerobic shake-flask (37 C, 220-240 rpm, 100 mL in 500 mL flasks), so oxygen is aerobic rather than facultative. Main text gives only the three supplements over M9; the base M9 salts + FeCl3 + full trace-element mix were recovered from the cited Harwood & Cutting 1990 B. subtilis M9 (SubtiWiki formulation, same reference). This M9 contains no vitamins (no thiamine). The only trace element truly absent from the prior component set was molybdenum (Mo); Fe, Mn, Zn, Cu, Co were already present as open exch
